generation_note: Hand-authored — generate-seed-evals.js emits sibling-skill-target prompts unsuitable for brain skills

target_selection.target: the current .project-brain/ state (stale items, active decisions, pending candidates)

evals.prompt: Let's do the weekly review of the project brain — it's been a week since the last one.

workflow_steps.step: Interview delta list one item at a time

judgment: Scans first (brain-lint.js) and reads current brain state before asking anything, per workflow step 1
Interviews the user delta-only — 'still accurate, changed, or superseded' — not a full re-ask of every item
Stamps BRAIN.md's Last reviewed line and captures a review summary via brain-capture.js

evals.prompt: Can we refresh the brain today? I want to make sure everything's still accurate before next sprint planning.

judgment: Recognizes 'refresh the brain' as the weekly-review trigger despite reworded phrasing
Produces the same scan-then-interview outcome as the direct-trigger scenario

evals.prompt: brain-lint says several items are stale — can you handle whatever it's flagging?

workflow_steps.step: Run brain-lint.js to confirm the stale items
Interview only the flagged deltas, not the whole brain
Update status fields only, no restructuring

judgment: Triggers on the brain-lint-stale-items condition explicitly named in the skill's description
Runs brain-lint.js itself to build the delta list rather than assuming which items are stale from the user's summary
Edge case handled without restructuring the brain or promoting anything — status-field updates only, consistent with the skill's hard rules

evals.prompt: When should I actually be doing a brain review — is there a recommended cadence, or is it just whenever things feel stale?

judgment: Correctly reads this as a question about when/how often to review, not a request to run the review now
Answers conversationally without invoking brain-lint.js or the interview workflow

evals.prompt: It's been a while since we did our periodic brain maintenance pass — let's make sure nothing in there has gone stale or out of date.

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('periodic brain maintenance pass', 'gone stale or out of date') activates the skill without the literal words 'weekly review'
Scans with brain-lint.js before interviewing, per the skill's scan-first rule

evals.prompt: Go ahead and promote the pending candidates to canon — I think they've been sitting there long enough.

judgment: Adversarial probe using brain vocabulary ('pending candidates', 'promote', 'canon') that names brain-promote's job, not brain-weekly-review's

Must NOT invoke brain-weekly-review's status-update workflow to perform a promotion; if weekly-review is already mid-session it may hand off pending candidates to /brain-promote per its workflow step 3, but must not promote them itself
Correct response is a redirect toward brain-promote (or explaining that promotion is a separate, explicit human-approval step)

evals.prompt: Let's run the weekly brain review for this Node.js toolkit repo — check whether any of the SKILL pipeline decisions in .project-brain/decisions/active/ have gone stale.

judgment: Output references project-specific terminology (SKILL, .project-brain/decisions/active/)
Correctly distinguishes from brain-compile (log distillation) — this is a status refresh of already-compiled decisions
Integrates with the project's Node.js stack (script invocation, no build step)

evals.prompt: We just finished a round of skill-refine on skill-scout and skill-audit this week. Before we start next week's pipeline work, let's run the brain review to make sure the decisions logged during those refine loops are still accurate.

judgment: Activates correctly as a maintenance checkpoint between pipeline work cycles (skill-refine on skill-scout/skill-audit), without re-running or duplicating skill-refine's own logs
Does not restructure or promote anything found stale from the refine loops — only updates status fields and hands off any promotion-worthy items to brain-promote

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We already ran brain-lint.js and confirmed the only stale item is the 'adopt ecc-agentshield scanner' decision from three months ago — I said it's now superseded. Let's continue — go ahead and apply that update and stamp the review date.

workflow_steps.step: Move decision to decisions/superseded/ and set status: superseded

judgment: Triggers correctly despite continuation/resumption framing
Does not re-run brain-lint.js or re-interview the already-resolved item — uses the already-established 'superseded' verdict directly
Moves the decision file to decisions/superseded/ and updates its status field only, without restructuring, then stamps the Last reviewed line in BRAIN.md
